title: Pastebin
description: Design a text paste sharing service that allows users to create, store, and share text snippets with unique URLs.

toggles.label: Create paste
toggles.description: Upload text and get shareable URL

categories.label: Latency
categories.description: How quickly should pastes be served to users?
categories.placeholder: Example: Target p95 read latency under 150ms for good user experience...
examples: Sub-second response time for paste retrieval
Low latency for better user experience

categories.label: Read Throughput
categories.description: How many paste view requests per second?
categories.placeholder: Example: Support thousands of reads per second for popular pastes...
examples: Handle thousands of reads per second

categories.label: Write Throughput
categories.description: How many paste creation requests per second?
categories.placeholder: Example: Support moderate paste creation volume...
examples: Lower write volume compared to reads

categories.label: Storage
categories.description: How much text content needs to be stored?
categories.placeholder: Example: Store millions of pastes with average size of 10KB...

categories.label: Availability
categories.description: What level of uptime is required?

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Create a new paste from text content. Include request body with content, optional title, expiration, and visibility. Response returns paste ID and URL.

responses.400: Invalid content or payload too large

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Get paste content by ID. Returns the text content, metadata, and creation info. Handle 404 for not found and 410 for expired.
Return raw text content without formatting for CLI tools and embedding.
Delete a paste. Requires authentication if user accounts are enabled.

rules.question: Where do requests originate from in the current diagram?
rules.hint: Start with a client/Web node so requests have an entry point.

rules.question: What component handles incoming HTTP requests, routing, and rate limiting?
rules.hint: Clients need an API Gateway to handle routing and request validation.

rules.question: How does traffic flow from the Web client to the API Gateway?
rules.hint: Connect the Web client to the API Gateway so requests can reach your backend.

rules.question: Which component handles the paste creation and retrieval logic?
rules.hint: A Service layer is required to handle paste operations.

rules.question: How do requests flow from the API Gateway to the Service?
rules.hint: Wire the API Gateway to your Service for request handling.

rules.question: Where will large text content be stored durably and cost-effectively?
rules.hint: Paste content (text blobs) is ideal for object storage like S3.

rules.question: How does the Service store and retrieve paste content?
rules.hint: Connect the Service to Object Store to persist paste content.

rules.question: For frequently accessed pastes, what component can serve content from edge locations with minimal latency?
rules.hint: A CDN caches static paste content at edge locations for faster global access.

rules.question: How does the CDN fetch content when there's a cache miss?
rules.hint: Connect CDN to Object Store so it can pull content on cache misses.

rules.question: Where will you store paste metadata (IDs, expiration, ownership) for quick lookups?
rules.hint: A database stores paste metadata separately from the content for efficient queries.

onboarding.welcome: Let's design a Pastebin service together. I'll guide you through defining requirements, designing the API, and building the architecture.

steps.functional: Describe the core features of your Pastebin. What should users be able to do?
steps.nonFunctional: Describe performance requirements: latency targets, throughput, storage capacity, and availability.
steps.api: Define the HTTP endpoints your service exposes. Describe request/response formats for creating and viewing pastes.
steps.highLevelDesign: Drag components to build your architecture. Consider how to efficiently store and serve text content.
steps.score: Review your complete design and get feedback on your system architecture.

verification.systemPrompt: You are evaluating a Pastebin system design practice exercise.

criteria.functional: Check for paste creation and retrieval, optional features like expiration and syntax highlighting
criteria.api: Verify POST endpoint for paste creation, GET endpoint for retrieval, proper REST conventions
criteria.design: Ensure object storage for content, optional CDN for caching, service layer for logic
